name: West Cell
explanation: A west-coast complex-oscillator voice. A modulator sine cross-modulates (through-zero FM) a primary sine, then the result is driven into a series wavefolder that creases the wave and multiplies its harmonics, and finally a low-pass gate (a combined filter+amp) plucks the tone with a single fast control envelope for bongo/marimba/clang transients. Pitch tracks, plays chords, and stays bounded. Controls: Timbre, Fold, Gate Decay, Tone and Level.
